hints.text: Plates **short, white w/ blue text** — blue often invisible through the blur (looks plain white)
**Only Gen3 coverage** — solid white car, no antenna

shared_with.note: Same white antenna-less car — but Peru HAS Gen4.

shared_with.diff: Chile's black-section variant + all-white/all-yellow lines → Chile.

hints.text: Buildings of **large red bricks w/ an 'unfinished' look**

shared_with.note: Common near Lake Titicaca in Peru too.
shared_with.diff: White square signposts + Tigo ads → Bolivia.

hints.text: Middle lines **all-yellow, yellow+white mixed, or all-white**; outer lines always white

shared_with.note: Peru often has outer-lines-only roads — very rare in Bolivia.

hints.text: Lots of **concrete-slab roads** + **cobblestone roads** w/ neat stone lines separating disorganised cobbles
Guardrail markings w/ **yellow stripes on the OUTSIDE**

shared_with.note: Peru inverts it: black on the outside.
shared_with.diff: Yellow outer → Bolivia; black outer → Peru.

hints.text: Poles inconsistent: round concrete w/ pinholes + wooden (rarely Argentina-style); **extremely curvy tree-trunk wooden poles** (esp. east) = a very good Bolivia clue
hints.uniq_note: Slightly bent poles exist elsewhere — EXTREMELY curvy ones scream Bolivia.

hints.text: km markers: **black top w/ 'Km' / white bottom w/ distance — NO road number**

shared_with.note: Similar from afar, but Argentina's markers show a road number.

hints.text: **Traditional dress** common (bowler hats, colourful shawls, pollera skirts — also near-border Peru); **MAS/Evo political graffiti** + objects painted black-white-blue; **Wiphala** flag (7-colour diagonal patchwork); **Tigo** telecom ads (does NOT operate in Peru — solves urban 50-50s); rocks & soil often distinctly **reddish**
hints.uniq_note: MAS colours + Wiphala + Tigo are Bolivia's fastest urban tells vs Peru.

hints.text: Coverage very limited: only central/central-west — six main cities (**La Paz/El Alto, Cochabamba, Oruro, Sucre, Potosí, Santa Cruz**) have side streets; elsewhere main roads only (often partly unpaved)

**Altiplano** plateau dominates the west: grasslands w/ tufted 'hedgehog' grass, mostly flat roads w/ hills around; **lusher north (El Alto/La Paz), arid south (Oruro→Chile)** — exception: one side of divided Ruta 1 looks green up to Oruro; **Lake Titicaca** visible from Ruta 1 (Desaguadero↔Guaqui, lake north) + Ruta 2 (Tiquina↔Batallas, lake S/W), very green around

**Andes** (NW-SE) = tall mountains between Altiplano & Amazon; **east of the Andes** = green tropical flat farmland w/ characteristic **pink soil** (unpaved coverage only around Santa Cruz); **cobblestone roads** mainly in/SE of Cochabamba + around Sucre

bullets: La Paz: hilly, meandering green valleys; downtown = the ONLY 10+ storey towers; yellow-beige eroded sandstone pillars on hillsides (only here)

El Alto: flat plateau, dry & dusty, grid streets, endless unfinished red brick; wavy-edged grey brick pavement; **Neo-Andean** futuristic colourful facades (almost only here); gradient-painted gates (green→light green, red→yellow)
Cochabamba: flat valley w/ an imposing mountain WALL to the north
Oruro: driest big-city landscape, large dry hill to the west
Sucre: very hilly + round tree-covered hills; historic white-wall colonial centre w/ orange tiles
Potosí: hilly + arid, treeless hills; giant pyramid mountain **Cerro Rico** (mining-stripped)
Santa Cruz: the only big city in flat tropical surroundings (nearby towns look identical)
Ruta 1 west of El Alto: paved through green grass, white paint numbers on the road, Titicaca north near Desaguadero
Ruta 3 near Pongo (NE of La Paz): enormous dark-grey mountains + low clouds
**Death Road** (N Yungas): bendy unpaved jungle road, foggy south, cyclists — the only jungle dirt road
Ruta 1/4 La Paz↔Oruro: the ONLY large divided highway
Ruta 4 (Vila Vila→Cochabamba→Buena Vista): square smudge front-left
Morochata/Ruta 25: isolated Peru-lookalike mountain dirt road (eucalyptus west, arid east)
Ruta 1 Machacamarca↔Guindal: snow patches + gloomy overcast (north of Challapata: straight NNW, better sky; SE of Challapata: curvier, greyer)
Ruta 7 Mairana↔Santa Cruz: neon-like camera glitch to the north
Mataral area (Ruta 7/22): green low-vegetation hills, sunny; big cacti + yellow-flowered trees
Eastern Ruta 4 (E of Santa Cruz): flat green E-W, asphalt + concrete pads
Ruta 5 near Chaco (N of Sucre): sunset coverage on concrete
Ruta 4 SW of Patacamaya: worn grainy asphalt in semi-arid grass
Nevado Sajama (lone snow volcano) visible → SW Ruta 4 south of Callapa
Ruta 6 Tarabuco↔Zudáñez: near-white concrete (unique)
Villa Vitalina↔Coipasa: salt-flat + volcanic-cone desert drive

hints.text: **Sajama NP**: unique car w/ black roof rack + metal bars, unpaved road w/ fluffy bushes, Nevado Sajama always visible; Tomarapi village = adobe walls + thatched roofs
